input.problemStatement:
DurationField shows the wrong format string in its validation error.
Please update the user-facing message only.
Keep the behavioral surface unchanged.
input.predictedPatch:
diff --git a/django/db/models/fields/duration.py b/django/db/models/fields/duration.py
--- a/django/db/models/fields/duration.py
+++ b/django/db/models/fields/duration.py
@@ -10,7 +10,7 @@ class DurationField(Field):
-        'invalid': _('"%(value)s" value has an invalid format. It must be in ')
+        'invalid': _('Use [[[DD] [HH:]MM:]SS].')

goal: Update DurationField validation error message to show correct format string instead of truncated/incomplete message

hypotheses.claim: Format string '[[[DD] [HH:]MM:]SS]' is the correct Django duration format syntax
The format string bracket syntax '[HH:]MM:]SS' is correctly balanced

risks: If '[[[DD] [HH:]MM:]SS]' is not Django's actual duration format, users will be given wrong guidance
Removing '%(value)s' interpolation loses context about what value was rejected
Bracket notation '[HH:]MM:]SS' appears syntactically malformed (extra closing bracket)

issues.message: Cannot verify '[[[DD] [HH:]MM:]SS]' is the correct Django duration format - need Django docs or source verification
Patch removes '%(value)s' from message - user will no longer see what invalid value was rejected
Format string '[HH:]MM:]SS' has mismatched brackets - may be a typo, could confuse users
